---
title: "DATA3888 Volatility Modelling Report"
subtitle: "To be continued---"
author: "Finance 6"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

> version of the local image generated from the latest code on git

```{python}
#| label: setup

from pathlib import Path
import re
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, Markdown, Image

ROOT = Path(".")

CSV_PATHS = {
    "cluster_assignments": "plots/recluster/stock_cluster_assignments_FINAL.csv",
    "cluster_results": "plots/cluster_models/cluster_results.csv",
    "individual_results": "plots/individual_models/individual_results.csv",
    "timeseries_results": "plots/timeseries_models/timeseries_results.csv",
}

IMAGE_DIRS = [
    "plots/recluster/",
    "plots/cluster_validation/",
    "plots/cluster_models/",
    "plots/individual_models/",
    "plots/timeseries_models/",
]


def md(text: str):
    display(Markdown(text))


def placeholder(msg: str):
    md(f"> **Placeholder:** {msg}")


def choose_column(df: pd.DataFrame, candidates):
    if df.empty:
        return None
    lower_map = {c.lower(): c for c in df.columns}
    for c in candidates:
        if c in df.columns:
            return c
        if c.lower() in lower_map:
            return lower_map[c.lower()]
    return None


def load_csv_safe(path: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    csv_path = ROOT / path
    if not csv_path.exists():
        placeholder(f"Missing CSV file `{path}`.")
        return pd.DataFrame()
    try:
        df = pd.read_csv(csv_path)
    except Exception as exc:
        placeholder(f"Could not read `{path}`: {exc}")
        return pd.DataFrame()
    if df.empty:
        placeholder(f"CSV file `{path}` exists but is empty.")
    return df


def convert_numeric_like_columns(df: pd.DataFrame, min_fraction: float = 0.8) -> pd.DataFrame:
    out = df.copy()
    for col in out.columns:
        if out[col].dtype == "object":
            coerced = pd.to_numeric(out[col], errors="coerce")
            if len(coerced) > 0 and coerced.notna().mean() >= min_fraction:
                out[col] = coerced
    return out


def metric_columns(df: pd.DataFrame, metric: str):
    return [c for c in df.columns if metric.lower() in c.lower()]


def clean_model_name(col_name: str, metric: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(rf"[\s_]*{metric}\b", "", col_name, flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()


def best_model_by_row(df: pd.DataFrame, metric: str, id_candidates):
    if df.empty:
        placeholder(f"Cannot compute best-by-row table: input dataframe for {metric} is empty.")
        return pd.DataFrame()

    cols = metric_columns(df, metric)
    if not cols:
        placeholder(f"No `{metric}` columns found for best-by-row summary.")
        return pd.DataFrame()

    numeric = df[cols].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce")
    valid = numeric.dropna(how="all")
    if valid.empty:
        placeholder(f"`{metric}` columns exist but contain no numeric values.")
        return pd.DataFrame()

    out = pd.DataFrame(index=valid.index)
    id_col = choose_column(df, id_candidates)
    if id_col:
        out[id_col] = df.loc[valid.index, id_col].values

    out[f"Best {metric} Model"] = valid.idxmin(axis=1).map(lambda x: clean_model_name(x, metric))
    out[f"Best {metric}"] = valid.min(axis=1)
    return out


def metric_summary(df: pd.DataFrame, metrics=("RMSE", "QLIKE")):
    if df.empty:
        return pd.DataFrame()

    rows = []
    for metric in metrics:
        for col in metric_columns(df, metric):
            series = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors="coerce")
            rows.append(
                {
                    "model": clean_model_name(col, metric),
                    "metric": metric,
                    "mean": series.mean(),
                    "median": series.median(),
                    "count": int(series.notna().sum()),
                }
            )
    if not rows:
        return pd.DataFrame()

    out = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(["metric", "mean"], ascending=[True, True])
    return out


def show_pngs_safe(directory: str, width: int = 900):
    folder = ROOT / directory
    if not folder.exists():
        placeholder(f"Missing image directory `{directory}`.")
        return

    pngs = sorted(folder.glob("*.png"))
    if not pngs:
        placeholder(f"No PNG files found in `{directory}`.")
        return

    for img_path in pngs:
        md(f"*`{img_path.as_posix()}`*")
        try:
            display(Image(filename=str(img_path), width=width))
        except Exception as exc:
            placeholder(f"Could not render image `{img_path.as_posix()}`: {exc}")


def file_status_table():
    rows = []
    for name, path in CSV_PATHS.items():
        p = ROOT / path
        rows.append(
            {
                "type": "csv",
                "name": name,
                "path": path,
                "exists": p.exists(),
                "size_bytes": p.stat().st_size if p.exists() else None,
            }
        )
    for d in IMAGE_DIRS:
        p = ROOT / d
        png_count = len(list(p.glob("*.png"))) if p.exists() else 0
        rows.append(
            {
                "type": "image_dir",
                "name": Path(d).name,
                "path": d,
                "exists": p.exists(),
                "size_bytes": png_count,
            }
        )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


data = {name: load_csv_safe(path) for name, path in CSV_PATHS.items()}
cluster_assignments = data["cluster_assignments"]
cluster_results = data["cluster_results"]
individual_results = data["individual_results"]
timeseries_results = data["timeseries_results"]
```

## Introduction

The financial market is changing rapidly. Short-term fluctuations in the stock market play an important role in trading, risk management and other financial fields. Volatility forecasting is to predict the possible direction and the fluctuation of the stock market in the future. More accurate forecasting helps financial participations better manage risks and make wiser trading actions.

The goal of the project is not only to generate more accurate stock forecast results, but also to develop a practical stock forecasting tool that is easy for clients to understand and use. This project has developed a dashboard interface. Clients can clearly and intuitively configure the model through the dashboard, view the trend of individual stocks, and use suitable models for different stocks. The overall goal of this project is to create a practical stock volatility forecasting process to strike a balance between the accuracy, interpretability and practicality of the forecast.

## Background and Data

> To be continue---

## Methods

### Feature Engineering

> To be continue--- describe feature construction choices why here

### Stock Clustering

clustering figures from

```{python}
#| label: methods-clustering-figures

show_pngs_safe("plots/recluster/")
```

```{python}
#| label: methods-clustering-summary

if cluster_assignments.empty:
    placeholder("Cluster assignment summary unavailable.")
else:
    cluster_col = choose_column(cluster_assignments, ["cluster", "Cluster"])
    stock_col = choose_column(cluster_assignments, ["stock_id", "Stock", "stock"])
    if cluster_col is None:
        placeholder("Could not find a cluster column in cluster assignments CSV.")
    else:
        summary = (
            cluster_assignments
            .groupby(cluster_col, dropna=False)
            .size()
            .reset_index(name="n_stocks")
            .sort_values("n_stocks", ascending=False)
        )
        summary
```

### Forecasting Models

> To be continue--- summarise model families used

### Evaluation Metrics

> To be continue--- the evaluation metrics used for comparison(rmse,qlike)

## Results

### Cluster Validation

```{python}
#| label: cluster-validation-figures

show_pngs_safe("plots/cluster_validation/")
```

### Cluster-Level Model Performance

```{python}
#| label: cluster-level-tables

if cluster_results.empty:
    placeholder("Cluster-level results are unavailable.")
else:
    cluster_results_num = convert_numeric_like_columns(cluster_results)
    md("#### Cluster-level results table")
    display(cluster_results_num)

    md("#### Best RMSE model per cluster")
    best_rmse_cluster = best_model_by_row(cluster_results_num, "RMSE", ["Cluster", "cluster"])
    if best_rmse_cluster.empty:
        placeholder("Could not compute best RMSE model per cluster.")
    else:
        display(best_rmse_cluster)

    md("#### Aggregate model metric summary")
    summary_cluster = metric_summary(cluster_results_num)
    if summary_cluster.empty:
        placeholder("Could not compute aggregate metric summary for cluster-level results.")
    else:
        display(summary_cluster)
```

```{python}
#| label: cluster-level-igures

show_pngs_safe("plots/cluster_models/")
```

### Individual Stock Model Performance

```{python}
#| label: individual-level-tables

if individual_results.empty:
    placeholder("Individual stock results are unavailable.")
else:
    individual_results_num = convert_numeric_like_columns(individual_results)
    md("#### Individual-level results (sample)")
    display(individual_results_num.head(25))

    md("#### Best RMSE model per stock (top 25 shown)")
    best_rmse_stock = best_model_by_row(
        individual_results_num, "RMSE", ["stock_id", "Stock", "stock"]
    )
    if best_rmse_stock.empty:
        placeholder("Could not compute best RMSE model per stock.")
    else:
        display(best_rmse_stock.head(25))

    md("#### Aggregate model metric summary")
    summary_individual = metric_summary(individual_results_num)
    if summary_individual.empty:
        placeholder("Could not compute aggregate metric summary for individual-level results.")
    else:
        display(summary_individual)
```

```{python}
#| label: individual-level-figures

show_pngs_safe("plots/individual_models/")
```

### Classical Time-Series Baselines

```{python}
#| label: timeseries-tables

if timeseries_results.empty:
    placeholder("Time-series baseline results are unavailable.")
else:
    timeseries_results_num = convert_numeric_like_columns(timeseries_results)
    md("#### Time-series baseline results table")
    display(timeseries_results_num)

    md("#### Best RMSE baseline/model per row")
    best_rmse_ts = best_model_by_row(
        timeseries_results_num, "RMSE", ["Cluster", "cluster", "Stock", "stock_id"]
    )
    if best_rmse_ts.empty:
        placeholder("Could not compute best RMSE summary for time-series results.")
    else:
        display(best_rmse_ts)

    md("#### Aggregate model metric summary")
    summary_ts = metric_summary(timeseries_results_num)
    if summary_ts.empty:
        placeholder("Could not compute aggregate metric summary for time-series results.")
    else:
        display(summary_ts)
```

```{python}
#| label: timeseries-figures

show_pngs_safe("plots/timeseries_models/")
```

## Dashboard and Visualisation Interface

The dashboard provides an interactive interface that allows clients to access and search various stock values more conveniently and quickly, providing more accurate and practical volatility predictions for different stocks. It enhances the usability, interpretability, and reproducibility of prediction results. Compared to static tables and charts, clients can more flexibly and efficiently compare and analyze stock volatility dynamically, improving the efficiency of comparing the performance of different models in stock prediction.
Specific functions

### Clients can analyze stocks using different models
Clients can compare past volatility and predict future stock market volatility through interactive visualizations
It provides mature evaluation metrics, such as RMSE(root mean square error) and QLIKE, to help clients identify more suitable models
Clients can simultaneously study and compare the volatility patterns of multiple stocks

### Interface display
```{python}
#| label: dashboard-figures

show_pngs_safe("plots/dashboard/")
```
Image above shows the main interface of this project.

### Integration with model workflow
This interface is integrated with the modeling workflow developed for this project. It reads the output generated by the prediction model and presents the results through a Streamlit-based interface. This integration allows the analysis results to be presented in a structured and interactive format.

## Conclusion

> To be continue---

## Appendix

```{python}
#| label: appendix-file-audit

md("### File availability audit")
file_status_table()
```
